title: Sauropus quadrangularis
intro: Sauropus quadrangularis, also known as Peruvian pepper, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands, characterized by its quadrangular stem and small, green leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions like fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Sauropus quadrangularis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Sauropus quadrangularis belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae and the order Malpighiales. It is a species of flowering plant native to tropical Africa. The genus Sauropus is part of the subfamily Acalyphoideae. The plant is a succulent shrub that is often used in traditional medicine. Sauropus quadrangularis is also known as the "tobacco wood" or "four-angled succulent".
uses: Sauropus quadrangularis has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and roots are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In some traditional medicine practices, it is used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of its medicinal uses.

parts: Sauropus quadrangularis is commonly known as the 'Tongkat Ali' or 'Longjack' in some parts of Asia. The medicinal parts of Sauropus quadrangularis are typically its root, stem, and leaves.
